title: The Arteries Clog in {city.name}
trigger: The {city.primary_industry_workers} union calls a wildcat strike, barricading the main arterial road.
description: Thousands of workers are demanding better pay and hazard gear. They have parked heavy machinery across the highway near {city.landmark}. The {city.media_outlet} is running live feeds of commuters crying in their cars.

options.label: Concede to minor demands and offer a one-time hazard bonus.
options.advisor_reason: We bleed 200M, but restoring order to the supply chain is a tactical necessity. Better to pay them off than let this circus remain on the streets.

consequences.follow_up: The barricades are lifted, but the city's budget takes a hit. The union promises to return next year.
consequences.city_reaction: Traffic is moving again, but my wallet hurts. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Send in riot police to clear the machinery.
options.advisor_reason: A kinetic dispersal costs only 80M and re-establishes the hierarchy of authority. The tear gas will sting, but prolonged insubordination is far worse.

consequences.follow_up: Tear gas fills the streets. The machinery is removed, but several police cruisers are flipped.
consequences.city_reaction: Nothing says good morning like the smell of tear gas. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Join the strike personally and blame the federal government for conditions.
options.advisor_reason: Surrendering our command structure for street theater is deeply humiliating. We save 30M, but validating this mob sets a dangerous precedent for future pacification.

consequences.follow_up: The mayor is seen holding a picket sign. The federal government cuts all infrastructure grants in retaliation.
consequences.city_reaction: Our mayor is protesting against their own city. Wild. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Youth Rebellion in {city.name}
trigger: University students set up a massive tent city in the plaza outside the {city.government_body}.
description: They are protesting a new municipal tax on cheap food and internet cafes. They have brought acoustic guitars, a very loud megaphone, and an endless supply of self-righteousness.

options.label: Repeal the new tax and wait for them to go back to class.
options.advisor_reason: Retreating costs us 80M and makes us look soft to these children. Still, dispersing them quietly avoids the optics of dragging students across the pavement.

consequences.follow_up: The students declare victory and leave a massive pile of trash in the plaza. The budget gap remains.
consequences.city_reaction: At least I can afford lunch again. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Turn off the plaza's free Wi-Fi and public restrooms.
options.advisor_reason: A bloodless siege strategy only costs 20M and naturally degrades their morale. Cut their communications and they will scatter without a single baton drawn.

consequences.follow_up: The students leave within 48 hours, but human waste is found in the decorative fountains.
consequences.city_reaction: Biological warfare against teenagers. Classy. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Blast classical music at maximum volume 24 hours a day to drown them out.
options.advisor_reason: Sonic warfare is an elegant, non-lethal dispersal tactic for just 30M. The collateral hearing damage is unfortunate but necessary for clearing the perimeter.

consequences.follow_up: The students start raving to Mozart. The surrounding office workers sue the city for hearing loss.
consequences.city_reaction: I haven't slept in three days because of Beethoven. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Divine Discontent in {city.name}
trigger: A prominent religious leader uses the Friday sermon to declare the city administration spiritually bankrupt.
description: Thousands of worshippers pour into the streets demanding the mayor's resignation. The {city.media_outlet} amplifies the sermon, causing panic among secular investors who fear a theocratic shift.

options.label: Publicly apologize and donate city funds to the cleric's favored charity.
options.advisor_reason: Paying 150M in tactical tribute pacifies a mobilized demographic before they become a militia. It is a distasteful appeasement, but it maintains the peace.

consequences.follow_up: The protests stop, but the secular base feels betrayed. The cleric effectively has veto power now.
consequences.city_reaction: Guess we know who really runs this town now. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Audit the cleric's tax-exempt properties.
options.advisor_reason: Engaging in an administrative offensive neutralizes their leadership structure for 50M. We just need to ensure our security perimeters hold during the backlash.

consequences.follow_up: The cleric is found to own three luxury yachts. The protests turn into violent clashes between supporters and detractors.
consequences.city_reaction: Holy war over a tax bill. Typical. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Start a rival state-sponsored religious sect.
options.advisor_reason: Funding a psychological operations campaign for 300M is an ambitious overreach. Creating our own zealots sounds useful until they realize we hold the leash.

consequences.follow_up: Nobody joins the state religion, but the marketing budget bankrupts the city. The cleric calls for a holy strike.
consequences.city_reaction: The mayor's new cult has terrible branding. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Access Denied in {city.name}
trigger: Wheelchair users chain themselves to the inaccessible stairs of the main transit hub.
description: The protest highlights that millions were spent on transit upgrades but elevators were value-engineered out of the final design. Commuters are sympathetic but annoyed they cannot get to work.

options.label: Promise to build the elevators and sign a binding timeline.
options.advisor_reason: Buying them off for 250M avoids the tactical nightmare of forcibly removing wheelchair users. Some targets are simply not worth the collateral PR damage.

consequences.follow_up: The elevators are built but break down weekly. The budget deficit grows. At least nobody died.
consequences.city_reaction: Finally some accessibility, even if it smells like regret. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Arrest the protesters for blocking critical infrastructure.
options.advisor_reason: It costs 30M to clear the infrastructure blockage. The optics of physically extracting them are abysmal, but an unauthorized perimeter breach cannot be tolerated.

consequences.follow_up: Images of police carrying away wheelchairs go globally viral. The PR disaster is absolute and immediate.
consequences.city_reaction: Arresting people in wheelchairs. We have hit rock bottom. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Install a giant terrifyingly steep wooden ramp overnight.
options.advisor_reason: A 30M improvised structural modification technically fulfills their demands while discouraging occupations. The subsequent injuries are not our jurisdiction.

consequences.follow_up: The ramp violates every safety code. Several people slide down it out of control on the first day.
consequences.city_reaction: It is not accessibility, it is an extreme sport. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Battle of the Bazaar in {city.name}
trigger: Riot police attempt to clear an informal market near {city.landmark} to make way for a luxury mall.
description: The vendors fight back by throwing rotting fruit and building makeshift barricades out of vegetable crates. The {city.primary_industry_workers} threaten a sympathy strike if the development continues.

options.label: Halt the eviction and relocate the mall to another site.
options.advisor_reason: A strategic withdrawal costs 100M but prevents a multi-front skirmish with the unions. We will regroup and secure the territory when they drop their guard.

consequences.follow_up: The developer sues the city for breach of contract. The market stays, smelling strongly of overripe papaya.
consequences.city_reaction: Victory for the little guy, defeat for the city budget. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Push through with the eviction using water cannons.
options.advisor_reason: Deploying the water cannons is a highly efficient 50M clearance operation. A brief, overwhelming application of force prevents a protracted occupation.

consequences.follow_up: The market is cleared but the footage is brutal. The mall is built but locals boycott it permanently.
consequences.city_reaction: Enjoy your empty store built on crushed tomatoes. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Incorporate the street market inside the luxury mall.
options.advisor_reason: Surrendering the retail zone for 200M creates an uncontrolled mixing of hostile populations. It is a security nightmare disguised as urban compromise.

consequences.follow_up: High-end boutiques are now next to live poultry stalls. The wealthy shoppers flee in terror.
consequences.city_reaction: Buying a watch while dodging a loose chicken. Incredible. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Shadows in the Streets of {city.name}
trigger: Undocumented workers march on city hall after a series of workplace fatalities are ignored.
description: The {city.government_body} is paralyzed. Deporting them would collapse the local economy, but acknowledging them angers nationalist groups. {city.media_outlet} runs inflammatory op-eds on both sides.

options.label: Offer anonymous safety inspections without checking legal status.
options.advisor_reason: For 80M we implement a soft pacification strategy that keeps the labor force functioning. Maintaining economic output takes priority over strict border enforcement here.

consequences.follow_up: Fatalities drop slightly, but nationalists accuse the city of harboring criminals. The tension simmers.
consequences.city_reaction: Doing the bare minimum so people don't die. Wow. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Launch a mass deportation raid to appease the nationalists.
options.advisor_reason: A coordinated extraction operation costs 150M and thoroughly cleanses the zone. We will face severe logistical collapse, but order and authority will be absolute.

consequences.follow_up: Construction and service sectors instantly collapse. Trash piles up and buildings remain half-finished.
consequences.city_reaction: Who knew kicking out all the workers would stop the work. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Grant them all instant municipal citizenship bypassing federal law.
options.advisor_reason: Openly defying federal jurisdiction for 50M is a declaration of municipal insurgency. I will need to prepare the guard for imminent federal retaliation.

consequences.follow_up: The federal government sends national guard to arrest the mayor. The city declares itself a sovereign sanctuary.
consequences.city_reaction: We are an independent republic now apparently. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Fare Evasion Uprising in {city.name}
trigger: A 50% overnight hike in public transit fares sparks spontaneous riots at bus terminals.
description: Toll booths are set on fire. The {city.media_outlet} blames the mayor's new luxury vehicle fleet for the budget shortfall. The {city.primary_industry_workers} simply stop paying and jump the turnstiles en masse.

options.label: Roll back the fare hike and subsidize the transit authority.
options.advisor_reason: A 250M tactical retreat pacifies the insurgent commuters before they burn down the terminals. We buy back the streets, though it bankrupts the fleet.

consequences.follow_up: The riots end, but the transport budget is entirely depleted. Maintenance is deferred indefinitely.
consequences.city_reaction: Fares are down, but the bus has no brakes. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Deploy transit police to brutally enforce the new fares.
options.advisor_reason: Authorizing a hard compliance sweep costs 80M and reasserts our dominance over the transit nodes. Baton charges are messy, but fare evasion is a breach of discipline.

consequences.follow_up: Arrests skyrocket. Jails are full of people who just wanted to get to work. Fare revenue barely covers overtime.
consequences.city_reaction: Paying extra to get baton-charged on the way to work. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Make all public transit entirely free forever.
options.advisor_reason: Surrendering 400M and abandoning all access control turns our transit grid into a lawless shelter system. It is a total abdication of operational security.

consequences.follow_up: The city goes instantly bankrupt. The buses become rolling shelters, but technically everyone loves it.
consequences.city_reaction: Free ride to nowhere. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Holding the Line in {city.name}
trigger: Residents of a historic neighborhood build barricades to stop demolition bulldozers.
description: A corporate developer tied to the {city.government_body} wants to build luxury condos near {city.landmark}. The locals are armed with paving stones and ancestral rage. {city.media_outlet} paints them as either heroes or anti-progress terrorists.

options.label: Cancel the development and declare the neighborhood a heritage site.
options.advisor_reason: Ceding the territory costs 100M but avoids a bloody urban pacification campaign. The barricades are well-entrenched, so a strategic pause is our safest maneuver.

consequences.follow_up: The developer pulls all investments from the city. The neighborhood is saved but slowly crumbles from lack of funds.
consequences.city_reaction: We kept our homes, even if the roofs are leaking. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Bribe the neighborhood leaders with penthouse suites in the new condos.
options.advisor_reason: Decapitating their leadership structure with 80M in bribes is a classic counter-insurgency tactic. The mob will naturally scatter without their commanders.

consequences.follow_up: The leaders sell out. The barricades fall. The rest of the residents are evicted and deeply embittered.
consequences.city_reaction: Everyone has a price apparently. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Seize the demolition bulldozers and give them to the residents.
options.advisor_reason: Handing heavy armor to the civilian population is a 150M failure of containment. We are effectively sponsoring a localized militia action against a corporate entity.

consequences.follow_up: The residents use the bulldozers to destroy the developer's corporate headquarters. Absolute anarchy.
consequences.city_reaction: Grandma is driving a bulldozer. {city.slang_reaction}.
